title: Horizontally scale placement table dissemination
body:
<!-- If you need to report a security issue please visit https://docs.dapr.io/operations/support/support-security-issues -->
## In what area(s)?

<!-- Remove the '> ' to select -->

/area runtime

> /area operator

> /area placement

> /area docs

> /area test-and-release
## Describe the proposal

Today, placement table dissemination (copy from placement to each sidecar) happens only from the placement's leader instance. It means that every sidecar instance must connect to a single placement service instance and that instance will copy the placement table on every sidecar via the streaming connections. The copy (dissemination) of the placement table might not be fast enough to copy to all instances and changes can pile up during the deployment of multiple pods, leading to sidecar instances having stale placement table versions and invoking actors on pods that no longer exist. For example: https://github.com/dapr/dapr/issues/7193

This proposal is to allow the placement service replicas (non-leaders) to also accept connections from sidecars.

### Option 1 (recommended):
- If a sidecar serves at least 1 actor type (response from `/dapr/config`), then connect to the placement leader, as-is today.
- If a sidecar will only consume the placement table to invoke actors, then it should randomly pick a placement instance to connect to.
- The placement service will operate as-is today for the leader instance. For non-leader instances,
placement will accept connections from sidecars too and disseminate the placement table when there is a new version, like it is today. The only difference is that non-leader instances cannot emit changes when a node goes up or down, so it is for "listeners" only.
- The connection from the sidecar to placement must have a metadata or additional attribute in the request to notify if that connection must be to a leader node - when that is required, the behavior is as-is today: connections to non-leader nodes must be dropped at connection time or when the leadership changes.

### Option 2:
Implement the same as option 1 but allow any sidecar to connect to any placement service instance (leader or non-leaders) and allow non-leaders to trigger placement table updates on the leader's placement table when nodes go up/down. This option is not recommended because of the extra complexity needed to achieve this - although it would be preferred when we decide to implement placement v2.
